meta.policy: identities are never merged; every measurement carries a source; proxy variants are separate records

drivers.name: Purifi PTT6.5M08-NAA-08
drivers.findings: Dedicated 6.5" aluminium-cone midrange, PURIFI neutral surround. Qms 6.7, Qes 0.33, Mms 20.8 g, Cms 0.87 mm/N, D 13.0 cm, SPL 87.9 dB@2.83V/1m, mech Xmax +/-14.5 mm. Pixel-extracted: IMD Figs 10/11 (Purifi conv. 94 dB/1m, ~+/-2 dB); Fig.6 SPL HD H2/H3 600 Hz-3 kHz (<~550 Hz hidden by legend; fundamental ref 94 dB so ratio = value-94); Fig.7 current-domain HD vs freq @2.83 V. Fig.9 (HD-vs-level @250/1000 Hz) NOT extracted: black gridlines collide with black H2 curves and the blue 250/1000 Hz curves cross ~95-100 dB, so clean auto-separation isn't reliable - needs manual digitization. Figs 3/4 (Bl/Le vs x) not logged.

drivers.name: Purifi PTT8.0X04-NAB-01
drivers.findings: X04 long-throw 8" woofer. n94 band Gm: 300–2.5k −55.8, 80–300 −54.6 dB (high conf). Holds midrange linearity through the midbass (H3 −71.8 @80–300). Level sweep @125 Hz: H3 slope only 1.51 dB/dB (sub-cubic) — distortion barely accelerates with excursion. @1 kHz H3 is level-invariant (slope −0.33). Displacement-limited max SPL 111 dB @80 Hz (single, 2π). At system level its case rests on Fig.10 IMD (30+255 Hz) under simultaneous load — swept-sine HD washes out vs the 6.5.

conditions.note: each tone 80 dB; y = dB re voice tone
each tone 80 dB; y = dB re voice tone

measurements.method: 0–28.3 Vrms (woofer) / 0–14 Vrms (tweeter); ratio-slope: quad H2=1, cubic H3=2 dB/dB
sealed rigid piston 2π 1m single unit at Xmax (modeled, not measured)

conditions.note: +6 dB for dual; bounds displacement not distortion

drivers.name: Purifi PTT6.5X04-NAA-08
drivers.findings: X04 6.5" (NAA-08, aluminium). n94 Gm: 300–2.5k −54.8, 80–300 −46.3 dB. ~8 dB behind the 8.0 in midbass at equal SPL (excursion tax, Sd 132.7). Level sweep @125 Hz H3 slope 0.69 dB/dB; @1 kHz H3 level-invariant. Max SPL 107 dB @80 Hz single. NOTE: this is the X04 woofer, distinct from the M08 midrange entry.

drivers.name: Purifi PTT5.25X04-NAA-05
drivers.findings: X04 5.25" (NAA-05). n94 Gm: 300–2.5k −53.7, 80–300 −43.2 dB. Cleanest small cone above 300 Hz but pays the excursion tax below. @125 Hz H3 slope 0.79. Needs 36% of Xmax for 94 dB @80 Hz. Max SPL 103 dB @80 Hz single.

drivers.name: Purifi PTT1.3T04-HAG-10
drivers.findings: 1.3" dome, 104 mm waveguide. 1–20k power avg: H2 −50.2, H3 −78.9, Gm −44.1 dB at 94 dB. H2-dominated, H3 at floor above 2k. CRITICAL: H3 @1 kHz grows at 2.25 dB/dB — −55.4 @94 dB but −43.6 @100 dB. Clean crossover floor at 94 dB is not clean at 100+. ~1.5–2 dB cleaner than HAG-01.

measurements.method: 0–28.3 Vrms (woofer) / 0–14 Vrms (tweeter); ratio-slope: quad H2=1, cubic H3=2 dB/dB

drivers.name: Purifi PTT1.3T04-HAG-01
drivers.findings: 1.3" dome, 104 mm waveguide (HAG-01). 1–20k power avg: H2 −48.6, H3 −70.7, Gm −42.5 dB. H3 @1 kHz slope 1.5 dB/dB (−50.9 @94 → −43.0 @100). Slightly higher LF distortion than HAG-10; both argue against crossing the dome low at high SPL.

measurements.method: 0–28.3 Vrms (woofer) / 0–14 Vrms (tweeter); ratio-slope: quad H2=1, cubic H3=2 dB/dB

drivers.name: SB Acoustics Satori WO24P-8
drivers.findings: Benign H2-dominant in-band. Exceeds 8.5mm Xmax below ~25 Hz at 91 dB -> 8.7% THD at 20 Hz (over-excursion). Stays pistonic to higher freq than SB34 -> better upper-midbass behavior; tolerates higher crossover.

measurements.note: 315mm @5.6V, nearfield 20mm @5.6V; matched 91 dB/1m; NF<=200 Hz + 315mm 200-600 Hz; pixel +/-1-1.5 dB
Displacement+thermal envelope — modelled, not measured. Plateau 107.5 dB.

drivers.name: SB Acoustics SB34NRXL75-8 (Norex)
drivers.findings: Larger displacement (Vd 508 cm3) -> ~3.5x lower single-driver THD than WO24P at matched SPL and ~7 dB more max SPL. Stays within 10mm Xmax to lower freq. Hard breakup onset >300 Hz: Voishvillo-weighted distortion exceeds WO24P above ~300 Hz -> cross LOW (<=300 Hz).

measurements.note: 315mm @2.83V, nearfield 20mm @2.83V; matched 91 dB/1m; NF<=200 Hz + 315mm 200-600 Hz; pixel +/-1-1.5 dB
Displacement+thermal envelope — modelled, not measured. Plateau 114.0 dB.

measurements.source: HiFiCompass IMD 30+255Hz 4:1, ARTA, pixel-extracted (sub-pixel, RMS-closure anchored, bias +0.09 dB)
measurements.note: Filename excursion label 10mm proven impossible (Doppler bound x<=6.2mm); true 3mm.

drivers.name: Purifi PTT8.0X04-NAB-02
drivers.findings: Same displacement as WO24P (Vd 219 vs 217) -> ties it at the bottom octave (both Xmax-limited at 91 dB, ~8.5% at 20 Hz). But far cleaner 80-300 Hz (lowest THD of the four there) thanks to the ultra-linear Purifi motor, and ~5 dB higher thermal plateau (350 W). Best 8-inch of the group.

measurements.note: 315mm @4V (HPF2 50Hz), nearfield 20mm @4V; taken as matched ~91 dB/1m (midband) per user; NF<=200 Hz + 315mm 200-600 Hz; pixel +/-1-1.5 dB
Displacement+thermal envelope — modelled, not measured. Plateau 112.6 dB.
Normalization: two-level interpolation 4V(93.0dB)<->5.6V(95.9dB), measured slopes — highest: interpolation between bracketing levels; ~100 Hz H3 ridge flagged as possible setup artifact

drivers.name: Purifi PTT10.0X04-NAB-02
drivers.findings: Largest Vd of the four (533 cm3) via 14.75mm Xmax on a 10" cone -> SB34-class deep-bass capability and the lowest 20-600 THD of the group (0.53%), cleanest bottom octave (2.2% at 20 Hz). Thermal plateau ~111 dB (corrected; sens 88/2.83V): above 80 Hz it sits just under PTT8 (112.6) and SB34 (114), but above WO24P. Some breakup high-order content >300 Hz under Voishvillo. Deep-bass-leaning.

measurements.note: 315mm @4V (HPF2 50Hz), nearfield 20mm @4V; taken as matched ~91 dB/1m (midband) per user; NF<=200 Hz + 315mm 200-600 Hz; pixel +/-1-1.5 dB

measurements.method: min(excursion Sd*Xmax*f^2, thermal Lm+10logPe est), 1W-sens basis
measurements.note: Displacement+thermal envelope — modelled, not measured. Plateau 111.0 dB. sensitivity-moderate (~85 dB/1W); Pe estimated 400W (datasheet TBD)

measurements.source: HiFiCompass IMD plot printed annotations (authoritative); pixel cross-validated to 0.3 dB
measurements.note: Pe corrected to 350W (was carried as 400W). Convention: HiFiCompass IMA rel f30 — validated.

drivers.name: SB Acoustics SB34NRXL75-8 ×2 (coherent pair)
drivers.findings: Lowest THD of the five across 20-600 Hz (0.45%) and highest max SPL (+6 dB everywhere, 120 dB plateau), at ~half the cost of one PTT10. THD reduction is H2-dominated; H3/H5 sub-linear so drop only ~3-4 dB. Costs 2x cabinet volume + 2nd channel. Beats single PTT10 on distortion, max SPL and cost for deep bass.

measurements.note: From single SB34 NF level series (1V=82dB, 2.83V=91dB). Per-harmonic level slope s_n measured: H2 1.03, H3 0.68, H4 ~0, H5 0.47 dB/dB. Dual@91 total = each driver @85 dB = single@91 - 6.02*s_n, coherently summed. NOT the (n-1) law (which over-predicts H3 by 7-15 dB - validated false here).

measurements.source: From single SB34 NF level series (1V=82dB, 2.83V=91dB). Per-harmonic level slope s_n measured: H2 1.03, H3 0.68, H4 ~0, H5 0.47 dB/dB. Dual@91 total = each driver @85 dB = single@91 - 6.02*s_n, coherently summed. NOT the (n-1) law (which over-predicts H3 by 7-15 dB - validated false here).

measurements.method: single SB34 + 6 dB (excursion 2x Vd; thermal 2x Pe + coherent)
measurements.source: model (excursion + thermal) — derived from single-driver series
measurements.note: Displacement+thermal envelope — modelled, not measured. Plateau 120.0 dB.

measurements.source: HiFiCompass IMD 30+255Hz 4:1, ARTA, pixel-extracted (cursor+RMS dual-anchored, bias +1.07 dB)
measurements.note: At 71% of Xlin; 3rd-order products dominated by suspension asymmetry.

large_signal data measured on SB17CAC35-4 (ceramic), NOT the NBAC-8 (aluminium). Same NBAC motor/suspension. Midrange thermal compression transfers; LF excursion does NOT (CAC sample measured ~2.8mm 1-way Xmax vs NBAC 5.5mm spec). 1-3kHz region differs (ceramic vs alu breakup). NATIVE NBAC-8 multitone data still needed.; Limiting: distortion (40-150Hz, 3-4% THD) + excursion (-2dB notch ~150Hz); Threshold: -2 dB compression OR 3% (-30 dB) distortion

large_signal data measured on SB17CAC35-4 (ceramic), NOT the NBAC-8 (aluminium). Same NBAC motor/suspension. Midrange thermal compression transfers; LF excursion does NOT (CAC sample measured ~2.8mm 1-way Xmax vs NBAC 5.5mm spec). 1-3kHz region differs (ceramic vs alu breakup). NATIVE NBAC-8 multitone data still needed.; Limiting: distortion (40-150Hz, 3-4% THD) + excursion (-2dB notch ~150Hz); Threshold: -2 dB compression OR 3% (-30 dB) distortion

large_signal data measured on PTT6.5W04 (paper), NOT the NAA-08 (aluminium). Same motor -> compression transfers. NAA-08 is spec'd to lower distortion, so these THD figures are conservative (NAA-08 should match or beat). NATIVE NAA-08 multitone data preferred when available.; Limiting: compression only (-2dB ~500Hz); distortion never limits in-band; Threshold: -2 dB compression OR 3% (-30 dB) distortion

NAA-08 is spec'd to lower distortion, so these THD figures are conservative (NAA-08 should match or beat). NATIVE NAA-08 multitone data preferred when available.; Limiting: compression only (-2dB ~500Hz); distortion never limits in-band; Threshold: -2 dB compression OR 3% (-30 dB) distortion

comparisons.title: Compression at matched SPL — SB17 vs PTT6.5
comparisons.type: Compression vs frequency at matched nominal SPL
comparisons.method: EAC dense multitone; compression magnitude (dB), less-negative = less compression
comparisons.note: Compression magnitude (dB) at matched nominal SPL. + marks lower compression. Proxy variants (CAC ceramic / paper W04), NOT native NBAC/NAA-08. Extensible: add a driver key under any level block, or add a new level block, and it plots automatically.
